sglang.title: Multi-node Tensor Parallelism
sglang.author: Ying1123
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Similar to torchrun, enter command into each node with respective node-rank.
GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth0 python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-70B-Instruct --tp 16 --nccl-init-addr 172.16.5.82:20000 --nnodes 2 --node-rank 0

GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth0 python3 -m sglang.launch

vllm.author: garg-amit
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The naming around rope scaling type is confusing. Making correction to give the correct name reference and acknowledge the source of the method.
https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.13753
Models tested before this PR:

microsoft/Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct
microsoft/Phi-3-mini-128k-instruct

vllm.title: Correct alignment in the seq_len diagram.
vllm.author: CharlesRiggins
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR corrects a minor formatting issue in the documentation for context_len, query_len, and seq_len. There is seemingly an extra dash in the seq_len line, causing a slight misalignment in the diagram. This misalignment could potentially confuse users trying to understand the definitions.

vllm.title: [Kernel] `compressed-tensors` marlin 24 support
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Adds an additional CompressedTensorsScheme to supoprt w4a16 models with 24 sparsity, CompressedTensors24
Add support for w4a16 models with 24 sparsity quantized through sparseml and saved through compressed-tensors
Leverages the kernels (gptq_marlin_24_gemm) used for gptq_marlin-24

vllm.title: [Hardware][Intel GPU]Add Initial Intel GPU(XPU) inference backend
vllm.author: jikunshang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is first PR for RFC #3725
Intel is contributing both Intel CPU and Intel GPU support for vLLM. Initial PR #3634 for CPU already got merged.
This PR adds a new Intel GPU(also named XPU in pytorch context) backend to vLLM and supports the basic model inference feature, with FP16 type support.

vllm.title: [CI/BUILD] Support non-AVX512 vLLM building and testing
vllm.author: DamonFool
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hi all,
This is a follow up of #5452 .
It enables building with a AVX2-only vLLM and do a smoke test.
Thanks.
Best regards,
Jie

vllm.title: [CI] Improve the readability of performance benchmarking results and prepare for upcoming performance dashboard
vllm.author: KuntaiDu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Why this PR
Current benchmarking results contain too many information for people to digest. This PR aims to:

 Add workload explanations inside the markdown file
 Align the performance metrics and time units for different benchmarking results
 Only expose the key performance metrics in the markdown

vllm.title: [Optimization] use a pool to reuse LogicalTokenBlock.token_ids
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It is surprisingly effective.
Before:
Throughput: 32.37 requests/s, 16574.05 tokens/s
After:
Throughput: 35.64 requests/s, 18250.05 tokens/s
benchmark command: python benchmarks/benchmark_throughput.py --output-len 256 --input 256 --model meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf -tp 8
machine: 8*H100

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix KV head calculation for MPT models when using GQA
vllm.author: bfontain
vllm.bodyExcerpt: When using GQA with MPT models, a bug leads to returning num_heads rather than num_kv_heads for the number of KV heads for the model. This is due to the fact that for MPTConfig, the attn_config is a dict and not an object. Note that for DBRXConfig, attn_config is an object, so the implementation was

vllm.title: [Fix] Use utf-8 encoding in entrypoints/openai/run_batch.py
vllm.author: zifeitong
vllm.bodyExcerpt: NVRTC sometimes will reset system locale to ANSI_X3.4-1968 (NVIDIA/cuda-python#29 (comment), NVIDIA/Fuser#62 (comment))

vllm.title: [CI] Avoid naming different metrics with the same name in performance benchmark
vllm.author: KuntaiDu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I am developing the dashboard for vllm, and found that in current performance benchmark, different metrics may have the same name (like median TTFT and median ITL are all named median), making it difficult to parse the performance metrics merely based on the benchmarking results markdown file. So I
